title: campanula macrorhiza subsp. sabatina
intro: Campanula macrorhiza subsp. sabatina, also known as Sabatina Harebell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family. This plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Campanula macrorhiza subsp. sabatina include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
